extra.message: User input from request.form['expression'] (line 116) is passed directly to eval() on line 120 with no sanitization. An attacker can execute arbitrary Python code such as __import__('os').system('id').

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message: The address parameter from request.form['address'] (line 136) is concatenated directly into a shell command: rp('nslookup ' + address) where rp references os.popen (imported on line 4). An attacker can inject arbitrary commands using shell metacharacters, e.g. 'example.com; cat /etc/passwd'.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

extra.message: XML parser is created with etree.XMLParser(no_network=False, dtd_validation=True) on line 157, explicitly enabling network access and DTD validation. User-supplied XML from request.form['xml'] is parsed with etree.fromstring() on line 159. An attacker can read local files via <!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM 'file:///etc/passwd'> or perform SSRF attacks.

cwe: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

extra.message: User input from request.form['name'] (line 189) is embedded into a template string using Python string formatting (%s) on line 204, then rendered with render_template_string() on line 205. An attacker can inject template expressions like {{config}} or {{''.__class__.__mro__[1].__subclasses__()}} to achieve remote code execution.

cwe: CWE-1336: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements Used in a Template Engine
